hero.title: shelter
hero.subtitle: a safe place where your thoughts can breathe
hero.image_alt: machka writing her journal with her kitty ziggy

1.title: local storage & offline-first
1.description: keep your journals safe on your own device.

2.title: cloud sync (coming soon)
2.description: soon you'll be able to access your thoughts anywhere.

3.title: calm by design
3.description: no deadlines, no pressure. just a quiet space to write, at your own pace.

1.title: your thoughts are yours
1.description: shelter stores notes privately on your browser, so you can access them quickly, even offline. no one else can read them, not even us.

2.title: end to end encrypted
2.description: your entries are end-to-end encrypted and stored offline first. we never read, scan, or sell your data. period.

3.title: 0 trackers & 0 ads
3.description: we strictly never collect personal data. there are no trackers or ads following you around.

1.question: what is shelter?
1.answer: shelter is a secure place where your thoughts can breathe. it's a secure journal web app where you can write freely without judgment in your favorite browser.

2.question: is my data safe?
2.answer: yes. your entries are end-to-end encrypted and stored locally on your device first. nobody can read them, not even us. your data is encrypted with a key only you have.

3.question: is it open source?
3.answer: yes! you can find the source code on github and we welcome contributions.

4.question: what is this project inspired by?
4.answer: this project is inspired by the comment section of the song "shelter" by porter robinson & madeon. many people write daily entries there, and we wanted to create a personal, private space for those small slices of life. you can see the video here!

5.question: is it free?
5.answer: yes! shelter is free to use, ad-free, and open source. made with ❤️!

6.question: who is machka?
6.answer:
she is our official mascot! you can often find her and her kitty ziggy wandering around the website.

special thanks to fiddle for creating all the wonderful artwork!

start.title: ready to rest?
start.description: start your journey at your own pace.

whatAreCookies.title: what are cookies
whatAreCookies.content: cookies are small text files that websites store on your device to remember information about you. they're a standard part of how modern websites work.

ourApproach.title: our approach to cookies
ourApproach.content: shelter doesn't use cookies. there's no account or login system, so there's nothing to remember between visits beyond what's already stored locally in your browser.

items.advertising: advertising cookies to show you ads
items.analytics: analytics cookies to collect usage statistics
items.thirdParty: third-party cookies from social media or other services

localStorage.title: local storage
localStorage.content1: instead of cookies, shelter uses your browser's local storage to save your encrypted entries on your device. this is not the same as cookies, it's what gives you offline access to your content, and it never leaves your device.
localStorage.content2: everything stored locally is encrypted and only accessible by you.

futureSync.title: future: cloud sync
futureSync.content: we're working on an optional cloud sync feature, which may introduce a small number of essential cookies (for example, to keep you signed in). if that changes, we'll update this policy with full details before that feature goes live.

updates.title: updates to this policy
updates.content: if we ever need to add or change the cookies we use, we'll update this policy and let you know. we're committed to keeping cookie usage to the absolute minimum.

contact.title: contact us
contact.content: if you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us on the discord server

noCollect.title: we don't collect your data
noCollect.content: shelter is built on a simple principle: your thoughts are yours. shelter runs entirely in your browser, we don't collect, store, or process your personal data on any server. everything you write stays on your device.

storage.title: how your data is stored
storage.intro: all your entries are stored locally in your browser using encrypted storage. this means:

items.device: your notes never leave your device
items.read: we cannot read your entries, even if we wanted to
items.encrypted: your data is encrypted at rest with a key only you have
items.control: you have complete control over your information

analytics.title: analytics
analytics.content: we use self-hosted, privacy-respecting analytics (umami) to understand how shelter is used in aggregate — things like page views and general usage trends. this data is anonymized, never tied to your identity or journal entries, and never shared or sold to anyone. you can opt out at any time.

noAds.title: no ads, no selling data
noAds.content: we will never display ads or sell your data to third parties. ever. your privacy is not for sale.

cookies.title: cookies
cookies.content: shelter doesn't use cookies. there's no account or login system, so there's nothing to remember between sessions beyond what's already stored locally in your browser. see our cookie policy for more details.

rights.title: your rights
rights.content: since we don't collect your data, there's nothing for us to delete or share on our end. you have complete ownership and control of everything you create in shelter, and you can export or delete your data at any time directly from the app.

futureSync.title: future: cloud sync
futureSync.content: we're working on an optional cloud sync feature. when it launches, your data will be encrypted on your device before it ever reaches our servers, and only you will hold the keys to decrypt it. we'll update this policy with full details before that feature goes live.

changes.title: changes to this policy
changes.content: if we ever change this privacy policy, we'll notify you clearly and give you the option to review the changes. we'll never make changes that compromise your privacy without your knowledge.

contact.title: contact us
contact.content: if you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us on the discord server

welcome.title: welcome to shelter!
welcome.content: by using shelter, you agree to these terms. we've kept them simple and straightforward because we believe in transparency.

whatIs.title: what shelter is
whatIs.content: shelter is a private, encrypted space for your thoughts, notes, and reflections. it's designed to be a safe place where you can write freely without judgment or surveillance. shelter currently runs entirely in your browser — there's no account or login required.

yourContent.title: your content
yourContent.content1: everything you write in shelter belongs to you. we don't claim any ownership over your content. because your data is encrypted and stored on your own device, we can't access it anyway.
yourContent.content2: you're responsible for the content you create. while we can't see your entries, we ask that you use shelter responsibly and legally.

items.illegal: use shelter for any illegal activities
items.hack: attempt to hack, break, or compromise the security of shelter
items.interfere: interfere with other users' access to the service
items.malware: use shelter to distribute malware or harmful software
items.ip: violate anyone's intellectual property or privacy rights
items.superearth: swear eternal loyalty to Super Earth; any suspicious activity must be reported to your nearest democracy officer

availability.title: service availability
availability.content1: we work hard to keep shelter running smoothly, but we can't guarantee the website will be available 100% of the time. we may need to take it down for maintenance or updates occasionally.
availability.content2: since your data is stored locally on your device, your entries remain accessible to you even if shelter's website is temporarily down.

backups.title: backups
backups.content: your entries live on your device. we strongly recommend you regularly export your entries as a backup — if you lose access to your device or clear your browser data without a backup, we have no way to recover your entries. you're responsible for maintaining your own backups of important content.

noWarranty.title: no warranty
noWarranty.content: shelter is provided "as is" without warranties of any kind. we do our best to make shelter reliable and secure, but we can't guarantee it will meet all your needs or be completely error-free.

liability.title: limitation of liability
liability.content: we're not liable for any damages that result from using or being unable to use shelter. this includes data loss, even though we work hard to prevent it.

futureSync.title: future: accounts and cloud sync
futureSync.content: we're working on an optional cloud sync feature, which will introduce accounts. when that launches, you'll be responsible for keeping your account secure, and we'll update these terms with full details on accounts, synced data, and termination before that feature goes live.

changes.title: changes to these terms
changes.content: we may update these terms from time to time. if we make significant changes, we'll notify you clearly and give you time to review them. continuing to use shelter after changes means you accept the new terms.

contact.title: contact us
contact.content: if you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us on the discord server

credits.title: credits
credits.intro: thanks to everyone who has supported shelter in one way or another, whether with code, design, feedback, or just using it. this project would not be what it is without you <3

username.label: how should we call you?
username.tooltip: this is how we will call you across the app, you don't have to use your real name

devNotice.title: shelter is still in development!
devNotice.body: we're actively working on shelter. as a precaution, we recommend exporting your journal from time to time, just in case ;)
devNotice.export: you can export easily in settings › data › export!

quote.text: In the journal I do not just express myself more openly than I could to any person; I create myself.
quote.author: - Susan Sontag
